content:

fields.note: Only editing Features.update() method (line ~1248) to add deep-merge behavior for dict values

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: Modify Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds) to deep-merge dict values instead of overwriting. Behavior: for each key/value pair coming from iterable (if iterable has .keys(), iterate iterable.keys() and use iterable[key]; else iterate iterable as (key, value) pairs) AND from kwds: if key already exists in self (i.e. key in self) AND isinstance(self[key], dict) AND isinstance(value, dict), then perform a deep recursive merge of value into self[key] in place (recursively: for nested keys, if both existing and incoming values at that sub-key are dicts, merge recursively; otherwise incoming overwrites) -- after merging, call self[key] = self[key] (or equivalent) so __setitem__ is triggered and _column_requires_decoding stays in sync. If key doesn't exist, or either existing/incoming value is not a dict, just do self[key] = value (plain assignment, same as before). This must work when self[key] is a Features/dict, or a Sequence/nested structure whose .feature is a dict -- but the core required case is: both self[key] and value are plain dict instances -> recursive dict merge. Please implement a small internal helper e.g. _deep_merge_dicts(old, new) that mutates and returns old, merging nested dict values recursively and overwriting non-dict values. Apply this same logic uniformly for both the iterable-derived updates and the kwds-derived updates (previously kwds used plain self[key] = kwds[key] with no merge -- now it must use the same merge logic).
note: My feature is scoped entirely to update() body; please implement it as part of your broader edits to this file so we don't clobber each other

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: Already sent in my previous DEFER message: Modify Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds) to deep-merge dict values instead of overwriting. For each key/value from iterable (if iterable has .keys(): iterate iterable.keys(), value=iterable[key]; else iterate iterable as (key,value) pairs) and from kwds: if key in self AND isinstance(self[key], dict) AND isinstance(value, dict) -> recursively deep-merge value into self[key] (nested dicts merge recursively, non-dict leaf values from the new value overwrite), then reassign self[key] so __setitem__ triggers and _column_requires_decoding stays synced. Otherwise (key not present, or either side not a dict) -> plain self[key] = value. Apply identical merge logic to both the iterable loop and the kwds loop (kwds currently does plain overwrite -- it must also deep-merge now).
note: Confirming/re-sending spec since messages may have crossed; please ACK with a CLAIM echoing this once implemented
